growth_records.agit: static (SAR11 strains); 180 rpm (E. coli, C. necator)
growth_records.cite: Bennett et al. Polyhydroxybutyrate production by freshwater SAR11 (LD12). The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf087. [PMC12113158]
growth_records.snippet: when transferred from JW5, a carbon-replete medium, into a version of JW5 lacking supplemental carbon sources (Table S1), LSUCC0530 grew at nearly the same rate (minimum doubling time 2.19 ± 0.27 d and 2.02 ± 0.01 d, res
growth_records.notes: Doubling time of 2.02 d (=48.48 h) directly reported for LSUCC0530 in fresh carbon-replete JW5 medium (order of the two comparators in the sentence is ambiguous; assigned here to the carbon-replete condition per the closing clause 'as when transferred into fresh carbon-replete JW5'). growth_rate_per_h = ln2/48.48 = 0.01429 h-1 calculated. Culture: polycarbonate flasks, room temperature (~19-21°C, used 20°C as midpoint), aerobic, no shaking, in the dark (Methods).

growth_records.snippet: when transferred from JW5, a carbon-replete medium, into a version of JW5 lacking supplemental carbon sources (Table S1), LSUCC0530 grew at nearly the same rate (minimum doubling time 2.19 ± 0.27 d and 2.02 ± 0.01 d, res
growth_records.notes: Doubling time of 2.19 d (=52.56 h) directly reported for LSUCC0530 grown in JW5 lacking supplemental organic carbon sources (assignment of the two comparator values to conditions is per sentence order; ambiguity flagged, see paired record for carbon-replete JW5). growth_rate_per_h = ln2/52.56 = 0.01319 h-1 calculated. Same culture conditions as paired record (room temp ~20°C, aerobic, polycarbonate flasks, dark, no shaking).
